Tests the conversion of a boolean value to a string using the String constructor in JavaScript.

# Purpose
This code is a simple assertion test that checks the behavior of the `String` function when it converts a boolean value to a string. It verifies that calling `String` with the boolean value `true` results in the string `"true"`. This test ensures that the type conversion from a boolean to a string is performed correctly according to JavaScript's type coercion rules. The code provides narrow functionality, focusing specifically on validating the expected output of a single type conversion operation.
